other_observers: John Ealy (finder and homeowner), Steve Stachowiak
description: The bird was about the size and shape of a Townsend's Solitaire.  The crown, head and underparts were a bright yellow tending to hints of orange in the cheek and upper breast.  The chin, front part of the ears and very upper breast were black, and the black included the eye.  The bill was black of medium lemgth and slightly downcurved.  There was a large white upper wing bar/patch and a much thinner wingbar below.  The rest of the back was black, although when it was very close there were still some pale edges on the feathers of the upper back, which I took to be remnants of the winter plumage.  The tail was quite long and totally black.  I failed to notice the leg color.  The bird hopped around in the scrub oak, but did not go to the feeders, where John Ealy said it was going.  I did not hear it make any sounds.
